tracking.controllers: Index Knuckles / Vive Wand / Somnium custom

knownIssues.title: 144 Hz mode is extremely GPU-demanding
knownIssues.description: At 2880×2880 per eye @ 144 Hz, the required GPU output is roughly double that of DDR5-era flagships at 90 Hz. Even an RTX 4090 will need foveated/upscaling to hold 144 FPS in most titles.
knownIssues.workaround: Default to 90 Hz for graphically-intense titles. Reserve 144 Hz for simpler VR apps or titles with aggressive DLSS/FSR upscaling (e.g. MSFS with DLSS 3.5).

knownIssues.title: 130° FOV introduces optical distortion at panel edges
knownIssues.description: The wide FOV pushes pixels into areas the human eye resolves poorly. Peripheral sharpness is intentionally soft.
knownIssues.workaround: This is an optical design tradeoff for the immersion wider FOV provides. Foveated rendering aligns naturally with this — periphery is already perceptually softer.

knownIssues.title: Lighthouse 2.0 base stations required — 1.0 not supported
knownIssues.description: The Somnium VR1 uses Lighthouse 2.0 tracking only. Vive 1.0 base stations are not compatible.
knownIssues.workaround: If coming from an original Vive setup, budget for new Lighthouse 2.0 base stations.

optimizationTips.tip: OpenXR Toolkit fixed foveated rendering + FSR
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Somnium VR1 doesn't have eye tracking, but OpenXR Toolkit's fixed foveated rendering still recovers GPU headroom based on a preset periphery zone.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Install OpenXR Toolkit → Fixed FR (medium) + FSR Quality at 85% render scale reclaims ~35% GPU headroom. Not as targeted as dynamic FR but a major win on any high-res headset without eye tracking.

optimizationTips.tip: Use the Somnium runtime's native OpenXR path where supported
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Somnium ships with a native OpenXR runtime. Use it instead of SteamVR for titles that support it — lower latency and better panel driver integration.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Windows OpenXR runtime selector → Somnium. Confirm in HMD app that the native runtime is active. For SteamVR-only titles, fall back seamlessly.
